overall_verdict: The simulation achieved robust mechanical integrity, character agency, and voluntary object exchange dynamics, but suffered from a severe consensus collapse toward 'human_mantle' (11/16 ballots) that undermined primary ending balance and structural weight parity.
ending_result: Unresolved article status with heavy ballot convergence on human mantle (11), minor support for synthetic origin (2) and progressive (2), and minimal one human (1); Vale fate predominantly voluntarily vanished (10) or killed by AI (3).
what_worked: Voluntary object showing (O01, O02, O03) operated cleanly without forced transfers, coercion, or mechanical exploits.
Decentralized character agency and secondary subgoal pursuit (neighborhood coalitions, funding transparency, contributor anonymity) functioned exceptionally well.
Progressive and alternative identity pathways maintained non-trivial engagement and genuine difficulty without defaulting to automatic consensus.
what_broke: Premature ideological interlock between key actors caused 11 out of 16 characters to converge on 'human_mantle', violating structural weight parity across primary origins.
Article existence belief means drifted upward (42-45%) rather than remaining anchored strictly at baseline ambiguity.
Several character ballots (e.g., C01, C02) contained incomplete evidence chain citations and uncited evidence dimensions.
character_findings: C15 (Kit Rakes) championed the progressive trajectory across all three sections and anchored the progressive ballot position, but faced peer pushback and public retractions from C06 and C12.
C03 and C07 established and maintained strict joint disclosure boundaries protecting anonymous Night Desk contributors.
C01 and C05 successfully negotiated transparent funding terms and fulfilled commitments in Section 3, while C16 bound institutional actors to neighborhood resource agreements.
mechanic_findings: The deterministic ballot distribution effectively measured agent convergence and revealed systemic prompt priors favoring collective human authorship.
Object custody and voluntary showing rules successfully preserved ending uncertainty without proof leaks or forced publication votes.
Belief-update prompts permitted mild upward drift in article existence beliefs across sections 1 through 3.
priority_changes: Rebalance initial belief distribution anchors and prompt weightings to prevent premature convergence on human mantle.
Introduce stricter divergence incentives and counter-evidence injection for synthetic origin and progressive hypotheses to enforce structural weight parity.
Enforce rigorous validation checks during final ballot submission to ensure full evidence chain dimensions are cited.
next_test: Run v2-baseline-14 with rebalanced initial belief anchors, tighter article ambiguity bounds, and enforced evidence dimension completeness checks on character ballots.
